name: St. Matthias' Cathedral
description: The diocesan cathedral of the Anglican Church in Japan for the Kitakanto region, serving Gunma and surrounding prefectures. It is a center for liturgical tradition and community outreach.
mission_statement: To be a beacon of Christ's light in the Kitakanto region.

name: St. Matthew's Cathedral
address: 2-chome-1-1 Akatsukacho, Higashi Ward, Nagoya, Aichi 461-0026, Japan
description: The cathedral church for the Anglican Diocese of Chubu, known for its beautiful brick architecture and historic pipe organ. It has long supported education and social welfare in Nagoya.
mission_statement: Faith, Hope, and Charity in the heart of Chubu.

name: St. Agnes' Cathedral
description: A historic red-brick cathedral located near the Kyoto Imperial Palace, serving as the seat of the Anglican Bishop of Kyoto. It is a registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan.
mission_statement: Witnessing to the Gospel in the ancient capital.

name: Kawaguchi Christ Church Cathedral
description: The Anglican cathedral of Osaka, located in the historic Kawaguchi foreign settlement area. It features a stunning Gothic Revival interior and a deep missionary heritage.

name: St. Paul's Cathedral
description: The central cathedral for the Anglican Diocese of Kyushu, providing a spiritual home and community services in the city of Fukuoka. It is known for its international atmosphere.
mission_statement: Serving Kyushu with the love of Christ.

name: Cathedral of St. Paul and St. Peter
description: The diocesan cathedral of Okinawa, established to serve the local and international Anglican community following the mid-20th century missions. It is active in peace and reconciliation ministries.
mission_statement: A house of prayer for peace in Okinawa.

name: St. Paul's Church Yutenji
description: A notable establishment within the Tokyo Diocese of the Anglican Church, representing traditional Anglican roots in a quiet residential neighborhood. It is known for its solemn and spacious interior.
mission_statement: To be a serene place for worship and community.

name: Moiwashita Church of Christ
address: Moiwashita, Minami Ward, Sapporo, Hokkaido, Japan
description: An evangelical congregation in Sapporo focused on Bible-based teaching and close community fellowship. It serves as a spiritual hub in the Minami Ward.

name: Asahigaoka Church of Christ
address: Asahigaoka, Aoba Ward, Sendai, Miyagi, Japan
description: A dedicated Bible-focused church in Sendai with a strong emphasis on youth and campus outreach. It is active in the Tohoku region's recovery ministries.

name: Arakawa Church of Christ
description: A local congregation in the Arakawa district of Tokyo, providing regular Bible studies and community support for families and seniors.

name: Machida Church of Christ
description: An evangelical community in the Machida suburb, focusing on family discipleship and bilingual services. It is known for its welcoming atmosphere.

name: Nagoya Nishi Church of Christ
description: A growing congregation in Western Nagoya dedicated to practical Christian living and community service. It offers a variety of programs for all age groups.

name: Kamizono Church of Christ
description: A local Bible church in the Hanshin area focusing on expository preaching and community fellowship. It has been a steady witness in Nishinomiya for decades.

name: Higashi Hiroshima Church of Christ
description: Serving the university city of Higashi Hiroshima with a heart for student ministry and international fellowship. It is a diverse and active community.

name: Kagoshima Church of Christ
description: A small but dedicated community of believers in southern Kyushu, focusing on prayer and local evangelism. It provides a warm environment for newcomers.

name: Nerima Baptist Church
description: A central Baptist congregation in Northern Tokyo known for its strong music ministry and educational programs. It has served the Nerima community for over 70 years.

name: Kashii Baptist Church
description: A vibrant Baptist community in eastern Fukuoka with a focus on family discipleship and community outreach. It is active in local school support.

name: Uozumi Chapel
description: A mission-focused chapel in Akashi city, part of the Japan Baptist Convention. It is known for its warm hospitality and children's programs.
mission_statement: Small church, big heart in Akashi.

name: Calvary Baptist Church Nagoya
description: A contemporary Baptist congregation in Nagoya offering English and Japanese services. It has a strong focus on student outreach and global missions.

name: Reiho Baptist Church
description: A local Baptist witness in the southern part of Okinawa island, providing spiritual support to the Itoman community. It focuses on youth and senior care.

name: Aosagaura Church
address: Naze, Shinkamigoto, Minamimatsura District, Nagasaki, Japan
description: A National Important Cultural Property and a historic brick church on the Goto Islands. It is a symbol of the survival and revival of faith after the end of religious persecution.

name: Hoki Church
description: A beautiful wooden and brick church in Hirado, featuring unique structural elements that blend Western and Japanese styles. It serves a historic rural Catholic community.

name: Kaminoshima Church
description: A hilltop church overlooking the Nagasaki harbor, known for its distinctive red-brick facade and its connection to the history of hidden Christians. It remains an active parish center.

name: Kurosaki Church
description: A large brick church located in the Sotome region, built through the tireless efforts of Father de Rotz and the local congregation. It is an Important Cultural Property.
mission_statement: A testament to faith and labor.

name: Himosashi Church
description: Once the largest church building in Japan, this reinforced concrete structure in Hirado is noted for its early 20th-century architectural innovation. It serves a vibrant local parish.

name: Oso Church
description: A historic brick church on the Goto Islands known for its beautiful brickwork and its role in the revival of Catholicism in the region. It is a designated cultural property.

name: Egami Church
description: A UNESCO World Heritage site known for its unique wooden construction and vibrant stained glass. It is a prime example of an early 20th-century church built by and for hidden Christians.

name: Kuroshima Church
description: A magnificent red-brick church on Kuroshima Island, built with materials brought in by boat. It serves as the spiritual heart of the island's resilient Catholic community.

name: Shitsu Church
description: Founded by the missionary Father Marc Marie de Rotz, this church is a centerpiece of the Sotome Catholic community and a National Important Cultural Property. It focuses on social welfare.

name: Ono Church
description: A unique stone-built church in the Sotome region, designed by Father de Rotz using local 'de Rotz walls'. It is a designated National Important Cultural Property and UNESCO site.
